title: NOC 75110 – Construction trades helpers and labourers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 75110 professionals are the essential multi-skilled force that powers Canada’s building and infrastructure projects. As a TEER 5 occupation, these workers assist skilled tradespersons and perform a wide range of laboring activities on construction sites, in quarries, and in surface mines. In 2026, with the Canadian government’s aggressive Housing Accelerator Fund and multi-billion dollar transit expansions, construction labourers are classified as "High-Impact Infrastructure Talent." For immigration, this role is a key target for the Ontario OINP In-Demand Skills stream and the Nova Scotia Critical Construction Worker Pilot.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level5Major Group75 – Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourersSub-Major Group751 – Helpers and labourersMinor Group7511 – Trades helpers and labourersNOC Code75110

duties: Material Handling: Load and unload construction materials and move them to designated work areas using wheelbarrows or hand trucks.
Site Preparation: Erect and dismantle concrete forms, scaffolding, ramps, catwalks, and barricades required at construction sites.
Trade Assistance: Assist skilled tradespersons like carpenters, bricklayers, and roofers by fetching tools and holding materials.
Technical Support: Assist heavy equipment operators by securing attachments and providing hand signals to guide machinery movement.
Maintenance: Level earth to fine grade specifications using rakes and shovels; remove rubble and debris to maintain a clean and safe work environment.
Specialized Labour: Operate pneumatic hammers, vibrators, and tampers as directed by supervisors.
Safety: Direct traffic at or near construction sites to ensure the safety of workers and the public.
employment_requirements: No formal education is usually required.
On-the-Job Training: Extensive on-the-job training is typically provided by the employer.

Certifications (Role Specific):Traffic Control Certificate is often required for flagpersons.Rigging Certificate may be required for riggers and slingers.WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) and Fall Protection are standard safety requirements.
Traffic Control Certificate is often required for flagpersons.
Rigging Certificate may be required for riggers and slingers.
WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) and Fall Protection are standard safety requirements.
Physical strength, stamina for outdoor work in all weather conditions, and a safety-conscious attitude are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, labourers working on unionized commercial sites or in remote mining locations often earn $65,000–$80,000 annually when factoring in significant overtime.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the sector is undergoing a technology shift—including the use of wearable sensors for safety and 3D-printed building components—but the core demand for manual assistance remains high. As nearly 20% of the current workforce moves into management or skilled trades by 2030, the "Helper" level remains the primary entry point for the next generation of builders.
labour_market_demand: The demand for construction labourers in 2026 is Strong to Very Good, especially in the Atlantic and Central provinces. In Nova Scotia and Prince Edward Island, the outlook is rated as "Good" due to record-high immigration and internal migration driving housing demand. While British Columbia and Manitoba show "Limited" growth due to higher interest rates impacting residential starts, the Non-Residential sector (hospitals, AI data centers, and clean energy) continues to face a major labor shortage. Employers are increasingly utilizing the Temporary Foreign Worker Program (TFWP) to fill entry-level gaps.
Entry-Level Accessibility: Start your Canadian career with no formal education requirements and gain hands-on experience in the trades.
Red Seal Progression: Many labourers successfully transition into the Construction Craft Worker Red Seal trade, which offers interprovincial mobility and significantly higher wages.
Diverse Work Settings: Work on everything from residential high-rises and bridges to remote surface mines and oil & gas pipelines.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 5 role is uniquely eligible for several Provincial Nominee Programs (PNP) that specifically target "In-Demand" construction roles.
pathways: OINP In-Demand Skills Stream: Specifically targets NOC 75110 for job offers outside the Greater Toronto Area (GTA).
Nova Scotia Skilled Worker Stream: Includes a dedicated pathway for construction labourers with a valid job offer and 6 months of experience with that employer.
PEI Occupations in Demand: A direct route for labourers with a year of related experience and a full-time job offer on the island.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure targeting 33,000 workers already in Canada, specifically highlighting essential construction and trade helpers.
